## Architecture
_System structure, component relationships, key abstractions_

- StyreneScreen base class (src/styrened/tui/screens/base.py, v0.16.0+): abstract _load_data(), _cleanup(), _on_error(), _loading_message() hooks. self.bridge property raises BridgeUnavailableError when None. 3-attempt retry with 0.5/1/2s exponential backoff. StyreneLoadingIndicator widget. Worker ownership: suspend/unmount cancels owned workers. All TUI screens inherit from StyreneScreen. ui_state/ package: base, nodes, sessions, daemon, config, mail, comms, page_browser, workspace modules. [2026-03-10]
- Yggdrasil + I2P integration (shipped v0.15.3–v0.15.4): DaemonAdapter ABC at services/daemon_adapter.py. YggdrasilAdapter at services/yggdrasil.py (DISABLED/ADOPT/MANAGED modes, admin socket JSON-RPC). I2PAdapter at services/i2p.py (DISABLED/ADOPT/MANAGED, HTTP proxy port 4444/4445). Config: YggdrasilConfig + I2PConfig in models/config.py. CAPABILITY_YGGDRASIL + CAPABILITY_I2P announce bits. MeshDevice.ygg_address + .b32_address fields. /meta enriched with ygg_address+ygg_port+b32_address. PageBrowserService handles NomadNet (RNS.Link), HTTPS, and .i2p (HTTP proxy) URLs. NixOS module lives in styrene-edge (not this repo). [2026-03-10]
- MeshDeviceTree (peer-browsing Tree widget) lives in ExplorationScreen (Nodes workspace) only. MY MESH groups trusted nodes (RBAC role ≥ PEER) by interface with role badges; OTHER shows anonymous nodes with truncated identity hash + auto-queried /meta (version/profile) via _fetch_meta() worker. /info queried on explicit 'i' keypress. Dashboard (Home workspace) has no peer tree — it uses NodeInfoPanel + ActivityFeedWidget only. See src/styrened/tui/screens/exploration.py. [2026-03-10]
- DaemonHarness (tests/harness/daemon.py): Spawns isolated styrened subprocesses for integration testing. Key isolation mechanisms: STYRENE_CONFIG_DIR/DATA_DIR/STATE_DIR env vars → temp dir, .paths-migrated marker prevents legacy data copy, config_path_override forces generated RNS config (prevents using user's ~/.config/reticulum), STYRENED_SOCKET for IPC. Factory: DaemonHarness.from_fixture("alpha"|"bravo"|"host"). RNS announce discovery takes ~15s on localhost TCP. [2026-03-12]
- YggdrasilAdapter wired into daemon (commit ac2bfa6): _start_yggdrasil_adapter()/_stop_yggdrasil_adapter() mirror I2P pattern. Duplicate YggdrasilConfig in services/yggdrasil.py removed — uses canonical models.config.YggdrasilConfig. Both adapters now have _find_binary() with search order: explicit config path → ~/.styrene/bin/ → system PATH → Nix store. Default mode for both is DISABLED. [2026-03-12]
- CopActivitySummary widget (src/styrened/tui/widgets/cop_activity_summary.py) replaces ActivityFeedWidget on Home COP screen. Coalesces raw IPC events into prioritized situation lines (max 6): NODE_ANOMALY → UNREAD → FILE → SECURITY → HUB → NODE_DISCOVERY. Transport-tagged via discovered_via prefix mapping (TCP, Auto, RNode, I2P, Ygg, Mesh, etc). Resolved situations dim then age out after 30m TTL. ActivityFeedWidget remains for Diagnostics tab raw firehose. 42 tests in tests/tui/widgets/test_cop_activity_summary.py. [2026-03-12]
- EventBus at src/styrened/services/event_bus.py — styrened's central nervous system. Simple async observer: subscribe(type, callback)/emit(type, action, **data). Five coarse types: node_changed, message_changed, hub_changed, link_changed, config_changed. emit() fans out via asyncio.create_task (never blocks). Auto-removes subscribers after 5 consecutive failures. Logging escalation: TRACE(5)=payload+timing, DEBUG=emit/sub, INFO=lifecycle+recovery, WARNING=slow(>500ms)+auto-remove, ERROR=exception+traceback. Daemon instance at StyreneDaemon.event_bus. TUI side: DaemonEvent Textual message (src/styrened/tui/models/events.py) posted from dashboard activity subscription, handled by screens for instant refresh. 60s reconciliation timer as fallback. Design doc: docs/internal-event-system.md [2026-03-12]
- Direct daemon launches read paths.config_file() -> ~/.config/styrene/config.yaml (see src/styrened/paths.py); editing ~/.config/styrene/core-config.yaml alone does not affect a running styrened daemon. [2026-03-13]

## Decisions
_Choices made and their rationale_

- Long-lived overlay canaries should expose a minimal styrened peer surface, not bootstrap only — A bootstrap-only canary would prove only daemon or peering reachability, not the end-to-end path styrened actually depends on. The long-lived canary should therefore pair the overlay daemon with a minimal styrened peer surface such as announce, chat echo, and /status. This gives clearer attribution and validates the full adapter→RNS→styrened path while keeping the canary intentionally narrow in scope.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/styrene-lab/styrened/docs/overlay-sanity-check-peers.md, decision: Long-lived overlay canaries should expose a minimal styrened peer surface, not bootstrap only [2026-03-13]
- Current canary scope is Yggdrasil, I2P, and Meshtastic; Lokinet and cjdns remain future candidates — The current code and design surface only make Yggdrasil and I2P real adapter-registry citizens, with Meshtastic already scoped as a separate bridge lane. Lokinet and cjdns appear today as provisioning/RPC placeholders rather than implemented daemon adapters, so they should be tracked as future candidates rather than first-wave canary commitments. WireGuard/MeshVPN is excluded from adapter-canary scope because it is a different service class with different semantics.. - Glitch convergence — character-by-character noise resolving to clean logo — Most cinematic option. Each character position has a randomized unlock frame drawn from a distribution weighted toward center-outward. Before unlock: shows CRT noise char (▓▒░█▄▀▌▐◆■). After unlock: shows final logo char. 30 frames @ 50ms = ~1.5s total. Concurrent with daemon polling so zero added latency. Fits Imperial CRT theme.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/styrene-lab/styrened/docs/tui-intro-animation.md, decision: Glitch convergence — character-by-character noise resolving to clean logo [2026-03-13]
- Terminal font: document Tomorrow, render with JetBrains Mono Unicode compat — Textual cannot set terminal fonts — the emulator controls this. Tomorrow is already the declared font-sans in styrene_brand.py. ASCII art must look great in any monospace font (JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, SF Mono). We add a docs/TERMINAL-SETUP.md recommending Tomorrow or JetBrains Mono. The wordmark in the splash uses Unicode block chars that match Tomorrow's geometric, condensed, extrabold aesthetic regardless of terminal font.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/styrene-lab/styrened/docs/tui-intro-animation.md, decision: Terminal font: document Tomorrow, render with JetBrains Mono Unicode compat [2026-03-13]
- Warm-up actionability is per-adapter — no universal WARMING behavior — Different adapters have fundamentally different warm-up cycles. I2P tunnel bootstrapping is non-actionable — it's a probabilistic network integration process; the operator can only wait. A future Yggdrasil adapter might offer a "retry peer connection" affordance. The adapter registration interface must allow each adapter to declare its warm-up behavior (non-actionable, retryable, configurable-interval, etc.) so the COP widget can render appropriate affordances or none at all. No universal WARMING UX pattern is imposed top-down.. - Adapter status reflects actual probe reality — no inferred or cached state beyond probe interval — The displayed state must match the adapter's actual probe result. If the probe says READY (proxy bound, test fetch succeeded), show READY — do not hold in WARMING longer than the probe interval. If the probe says DEGRADED (proxy was reachable, now isn't), show DEGRADED immediately — do not linger in READY. Accuracy is non-negotiable: showing WARMING when the adapter is actually functional, or READY when it has silently failed, actively deceives the operator. The probe interval is the only tolerable lag.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/styrene-lab/styrened/docs/cop-adapter-status.md, decision: Adapter status reflects actual probe reality — no inferred or cached state beyond probe interval [2026-03-13]
- Adapter registration interface is a Protocol/ABC — each adapter class implements it directly — Each adapter (I2PAdapter, future YggdrasilAdapter, etc.) implements an AdapterProtocol ABC: probe() → AdapterState, display_name: str, warmup_behavior: WarmupBehavior. The registry is simply list[AdapterProtocol] assembled at daemon startup. Probe logic stays encapsulated in the adapter class, not in external config. Elegant, avoids overcomplication, and minimizes maintenance overhead as new adapters are added.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/styrene-lab/styrened/docs/cop-adapter-status.md, decision: Adapter registration interface is a Protocol/ABC — each adapter class implements it directly [2026-03-13]
- adapter_changed is a 6th EventBus top-level type — Adapter state is a distinct semantic category — service-layer, not transport-layer (link_changed) and not operator-infrastructure (hub_changed). The "no new bus types" constraint from cop-activity-summary was scoped to avoid inflating the schema for one widget's convenience; it doesn't apply here. A clean 6th type is better than contaminating an existing one. Actions: ready, warming, degraded, probing, disabled.. ## Known Issues
_Bugs, flaky tests, workarounds_

- ActivityFeedWidget EVT unknown regression (fixed commit 01bac99): dashboard._subscribe_activity() used event.get("type") but NotificationService wire payload uses "event_type" as the key. All activity events showed as "EVT unknown". Test was written with same wrong key, masking the bug. Root cause: IPC contract gap — mock didn't reflect real wire format. Canonical fix: Tier 2 daemon contract test (test_ipc_contract.py) would catch this class of key mismatch. [2026-03-10] [2026-03-10]
- Textual's show_locals=True in crash/snapshot reports captures os.environ including secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, etc.). Mitigated: snapshot_report.html added to .gitignore, StyreneApp._print_error_renderables() overridden to write to log dir. Incident documented in docs/incidents/2026-03-11-snapshot-report-credential-leak.md. GitHub Secret Scanning not yet enabled on the repo. [2026-03-12]
- Textual Footer and Header have DEFAULT_CSS height:1 with dock:bottom/top. Adding any border (border-top, border-bottom) to these widgets adds gutter that eats the entire 1-row content height, rendering them invisible (size height=0). The children exist but render into 0 pixels. Never add borders to docked height:1 widgets in Textual — use background color or a separate divider widget instead. [2026-03-12]

## Patterns & Conventions
_Code style, project conventions, common approaches_

- TUI test execution: run per-subdirectory, never glob `tests/tui/` — integration tests in `tests/tui/integration/` have 30s socket timeouts that balloon a 5s suite to 8+ min. Safe targets: `tests/tui/screens/`, `tests/tui/models/`, `tests/tui/widgets/`, `tests/tui/services/` (except integration/). Unit tests via `just test-unit` run `tests/test_*.py tests/unit/` only (~22s). [2026-03-10]
- Release rules: (1) Changes DO NOT EXIST unless part of an upstream tagged release — always commit, push, tag vX.Y.Z, push the tag. (2) After tagging, run `just publish` immediately to push to PyPI — do not wait for the Argo pipeline. (3) styrene (meta-package) and styrened versions are UNIFIED — bumping styrened auto-syncs styrene-pypi. Treat as one version track. Release flow: bump __init__.py + VERSION → commit → push → tag → push tag → just publish. [2026-03-10]
- TUI settings tests: tests/tui/screens/test_settings_tui.py — 27 async Textual tests covering composition, form interaction, validation, persistence, keyboard bindings, identity icon validation, IPC failure handling, and Network tab peer row CRUD. (Note: tests/tui/ tree, NOT tests/unit/tui/ — that path does not exist.) [2026-03-10]

### 2026-03-14: Started dev daemon for TUI testing
Goal was to launch a development styrened daemon in isolation so the operator could run the TUI against it. The system daemon was blocking (RNS singleton conflict), so it was stopped via launchctl. The dev daemon was then started successfully with STYRENE_HOME=~/.styrene-dev, initialized RNS, discovered mesh peers (Varx, Community Hub), and is now running and ready for TUI client connections. Implementation: when computing a peer's derived trust score, filter the endorsement graph to downweight edges from BLOCKED peers by one step before running the WLC propagation.. See /Users/cwilson/workspace/styrene-lab/styrened/docs/styrene-distrust-model.md, decision: One-step distrust model — distrust filters trust signals, never propagates independently
- No enemy-of-my-enemy transitivity — (distrust→distrust) has no transitive meaning — The paper leaves this philosophically unresolved and identifies pathological cases. An attacker can construct Sybil nodes that loudly distrust legitimate voices hoping to trigger false trust signals via double-negation. The rule is not implemented. A's distrust of B, combined with B's distrust of C, tells A nothing about C. The only valid distrust transitivity is the one-step filter: A distrusts B → A discounts B's positive endorsements once. Negative×negative=positive is not modeled.. - JXL (JPEG XL) accepted as native Styrene image format. Key factors: best compression at ≤50KB (mesh sweet spot), lossless JPEG repack (unique — 20% smaller, bit-exact reversible), progressive decode for slow links, pure Rust decoder (jxl-oxide 0.12, actively maintained), clean BSD-3 license. WebP rejected (less efficient at ≤50KB, no JPEG repack, pure Rust zenwebp only 0.3). AVIF rejected (patent complexity, 3x slower ARM decode, fragmented Rust crates). JPEG accepted as universal fallback.
- Image format alternatives assessment completed and documented at styrene-rs/docs/image-format-assessment.md. Rejected: HEIF (patents, no Rust), AVIF (patent complexity, slow ARM decode, fragmented Rust), QOI (worse than JPEG at ≤50KB), FLIF (dead), BPG (dead, H.265 patents), WebP (not best at ≤50KB, no JPEG repack, less mature pure Rust). WebP and JPEG remain accepted receive formats.

FIXED: replaced direct WG→bat0 bridging with VXLAN-over-WG per-peer tunnel architecture: bat0→vxlan-<peer> (L2/UDP)→wg-styrene (L3). Each peer gets a vxlan-<hash[:10]> interface added to bat0. VNI=7379, dstport=4789, local/remote=WG mesh IPs.
- mesh_vpn.py Issue 9 remaining: _peers dict accessed from RNS thread (handle_handshake) and asyncio thread (_configure_peer) — no asyncio.Lock. CPython GIL protects simple dict ops but compound read-modify-write is a race.
- MeshVPNService VXLAN-over-WG: VXLAN uses WG mesh IPv6 (ULA) addresses as local/remote endpoints, but VXLAN encapsulates in UDP which must traverse the WG tunnel — sending VXLAN UDP over a WG interface whose route is only the peer's /128 works, but routing of the VXLAN multicast/broadcast frames depends on BATMAN-ADV's own MAC learning, not on WG routing. The 'dev wg-styrene' parameter roots the VXLAN in the WG interface correctly.
- MeshVPNService _configure_peer() calls _create_vxlan_for_peer() only when self.config.gateway is True — non-gateway nodes never get VXLAN overlays, meaning non-gateway Linux nodes that connect to a gateway won't extend bat0 on their end. Only the gateway side creates VXLANs, leaving a half-tunnel.
- VXLAN for_peer uses ALL_VXLANS share VNI=7379 — all peers share the same VNI. On the gateway, all per-peer VXLAN interfaces have the same VNI but different remote IPs (point-to-point unicast mode), which is correct. However if two peers somehow share the same VNI with different remote addresses on the same VXLAN namespace, kernel behavior varies. The point-to-point (unicast remote) configuration avoids multicast but VNI isolation between Styrene fleets using same VNI is absent.
- _destroy_all_vxlans() iterates self._peers but only destroys VXLANs that match entries still in _peers at teardown time. If a peer was manually removed from _peers without calling _destroy_vxlan_for_peer, the VXLAN interface leaks and is not cleaned up.
- VXLAN UDP port 4789 must be open in the firewall on Linux gateway nodes for VXLAN frames to traverse the WG tunnel — the existing styrened.nix only adds WG listen_port (51820) firewall rule issue; no VXLAN port exception is documented or implemented.
- mesh_vpn.py Issue 10 FIXED: second gateway evicts first gateway's /64 allowed-ips via _update_peer_allowed_ips() before assigning /64 to new gateway. Old gateway downgraded to /128. _destroy_all_vxlans() called in stop(). _create_vxlan_for_peer() called from _configure_peer() when self.config.gateway and platform==LINUX.
- mesh_vpn.py: extract_prefix returns zero-padded groups via IPv6Address.exploded (e.g., 'fd73:0079:...') but config subnet_prefix may use shortened form ('fd73:79:...'). String comparison in handshake will falsely reject peers on same subnet if config prefix isn't fully zero-padded. Normalize config prefix through extract_prefix before comparing.
- mesh_vpn.py: no MTU clamping on VXLAN interfaces — VXLAN+WG adds ~110 bytes overhead, so VXLAN MTU should be ≤1330 to avoid fragmentation. LoRa-bridged links need much lower MTU. No MTU set.
- mesh_vpn.py: no bat0 existence check before 'batctl meshif bat0 if add' — if batman-adv is not loaded or bat0 doesn't exist, batctl fails silently and the VXLAN interface is left up but not bridged into bat0.

Existing installs that previously ran init_db() without these models will have the tables missing until their DB is re-created or migrations run explicitly.
- Reconnect debounce is global (single _last_reconnect_time) not per-interface — a reconnect on interface A suppresses all reconnects on other interfaces for the full 30s window, potentially silently dropping legitimate re-announces.

- crawl_site() fires sequential fetch_page requests with no delay between pages — holds RNS.Link open for the entire crawl; no backoff on link drop mid-crawl.

- Three separate SQLAlchemy engines created to same SQLite file in daemon (lines 398, 1043, 1208 of daemon.py) — three connection pools sharing one SQLite file risks 'database is locked' errors during concurrent crawl+message writes.
- CMD_PAGE_CRAWL_SITE IPC handler fire-and-forget via asyncio.create_task fixed in v0.13.24 (was v0.13.23 partial fix in handlers.py only). Returns {started:true} immediately, bypassing 30s IPC timeout.
- CMD_PAGE_CRAWL_SITE IPC handler confirmed fire-and-forget in v0.13.24: asyncio.create_task launches crawl_site() in background and returns {started:true} immediately. Crawl progress not observable via IPC.
- public-hub NomadNet pages (index.mu, connect.mu, about.mu, docs/) are persisted on the nomadnet-data PVC, not baked into the image — they were created manually in a previous session. Only demo/ pages are copied from the image at startup.
- Micron color codes `Fg5af0ce (6-char hex foreground) were being misread as grayscale when prefixed with extra characters. The correct format is `Fg50 for grayscale-50 or `F + 6-char hex for named color. Renderer now emits correct Rich markup for h1 (#5af0ce), h2 (#3fbfa0), h3 (#6a9a8e) headings and cyan (#5ac8fa) clickable links.
- UpgradeScreen 'Restart TUI' button removed in v0.13.18 — _finish_success() now auto-dismisses with True immediately after daemon kill, triggering automatic TUI restart with no user interaction required.
- Pages tab in MeshDeviceDetailScreen shows correct destination hash (af1ec9...) but page requests time out on /page/demo/index.mu — page browser cannot fetch from hub's NomadNet destination despite correct hash resolution.
- NomadNet announces from the hub's own NomadNet process do NOT get delivered to styrened's announce handler because they share the same RNS instance — RNS does not deliver local announces to local handlers. Remote nodes receive the announce over the network normally.
- Page browser 'Page request timed out' error on first attempt is transient — NomadNet destination path not yet cached in local daemon. Subsequent attempts after path caches succeed. Confirmed working end-to-end: /page/index.mu returns 963 bytes from hub's NomadNet.
- Page browser 'Link establishment failed' error observed in TUI despite end-to-end page fetch working in standalone Python test — root cause under investigation: daemon's shared RNS instance may have different link establishment behavior than standalone RNS.Reticulum() client instances.
- Page browser 'Link establishment failed' debugging: added INFO-level logging to page_browser.py _get_or_create_link() — logs path found + identity recall result, link object creation with initial status, and ESTABLISHED/CLOSED callback events including teardown_reason. Released in v0.13.19 to diagnose daemon vs standalone RNS link behavior difference.
- asyncio.Future() in _get_or_create_link() is created without explicit loop parameter — in Python 3.10+ this binds to the running loop. If RNS callbacks fire from a different thread (RNS uses daemon threads), run_coroutine_threadsafe scheduling onto self._event_loop is correct but requires _event_loop to be captured correctly at service start.
- PageBrowserService fetch_page() fails intermittently with INITIATOR_CLOSED (teardown_reason=2) — confirmed transient: same test code fails then succeeds on re-run. Root cause may be stale path entries after hub reconnection storm or RNS state from previous instances.
- PageBrowserService works correctly when run standalone with asyncio.run() + asyncio.Future + run_coroutine_threadsafe pattern — all link establishment patterns tested and functional in isolation. The intermittent failure in the TUI daemon context is not a code correctness issue.
- Hub RNS config has 10 TCPClientInterface peers (Dublin, BetweenTheBorders, ON6ZQ, Beleth, Chicago Nomad, Acehoss, Quad4, Interloper, g00n Dallas, Loopback) — any peer cycling its TCP connection triggers id() change detection, producing the reconnect storm logged in hub pods.
- Hub TCP reconnect debounce in v0.13.20 does not suppress 're-announce' triggers — _handle_reconnection calls are debounced but log messages and re-announce still fire ~20/10s on the hub. 'churn suppressed' log never appears, indicating the debounce path is not being hit.
- public-hub build cached old styrened version (0.13.16) despite Containerfile updated to 0.13.20 — pip layer was cached. Requires --no-cache flag on podman build to force fresh pip install.
- _finish_success() in UpgradeScreen kills daemon then attempts to start new daemon via _start_daemon() before TUI re-exec — _start_daemon() uses shutil.which('styrened') which may not find the binary in pipx venv PATH, causing silent failure and TUI re-launch into DaemonSetupScreen. Under investigation in v0.13.21.
- After successful upgrade (v0.13.21), TUI kills old daemon, attempts to start new daemon via Popen, then re-execs. If shutil.which('styrened') fails to locate the binary (pipx venv PATH issue), daemon never starts and re-launched TUI shows DaemonSetupScreen instead of connecting to running daemon.
- Pages tab in MeshDeviceDetailScreen was incorrectly shown for all devices in v0.13.9 — corrected in same session to only show for NomadNet nodes or devices whose RNS identity also has a NomadNet announce (via node store lookup by identity_hash).
- CMD_PAGE_REGENERATE IPC command implemented: CmdPageRegenerateRequest in messages.py, handle_cmd_page_regenerate() in handlers.py, page_regenerate_index() in client.py, registered in server.py dispatch table. PageServerService.regenerate_index() is now exposed via IPC.
- page_server.py 'About Styrene' section fixed in v0.13.12: docs URL corrected to https://docs.styrene.io (was /styrened/api/ subdirectory), install recommendation changed to 'pipx install styrene'.
- fetch_changelog() PyPI fallback (Strategy 4 previously Strategy 3) fired for pipx users because the package is not installed from a git checkout, returning only version numbers with no commit messages — anemic changelog. Fixed in v0.13.13 by embedding _changelog.py at build time (Strategy 1).
- public-hub justfile had STYRENED_VERSION hardcoded to 0.10.21 in the build recipe (not just Containerfile default ARG) — both locations must be kept in sync when bumping versions. Fixed for 0.13.13.
- Pages tab missing in MeshDeviceDetailScreen when navigating from StyreneFleetTable (Styrene tab in ExplorationScreen) — _resolve_nomadnet_destination() fails to find the NomadNet announce entry because the hub's NomadNet destination hash differs from the Styrene node hash, requiring identity_hash-based NodeStore lookup that may not be finding the entry.
- _resolve_nomadnet_destination() searched only NodeStore but NomadNet and Styrene on the same hub use different RNS identities (separate ~/.nomadnetwork/storage/identity vs ~/.styrene/identity), so identity_hash matching across sibling aspects never finds a match. Live discovered_devices is also unchecked in IPC mode (always empty). Root cause: identity_hash assumption is wrong for multi-process hubs.
- _resolve_nomadnet_destination() resolution order: (1) device is NOMADNET_NODE directly, (2) device advertises 'pages' capability — compute NomadNet destination from RNS.Identity.recall + RNS.Destination.hash, (3) NodeStore identity_hash cross-match. Strategy 2 only works when styrened owns the destination (same identity). Hub multi-process case now fixed via 8th announce field (nomadnet_dest).
- Multiple unit test files (test_announce_fingerprint.py, test_daemon_announce.py, test_parse_announce_autoreply.py, test_short_name.py) have stale tuple unpacking expecting 7-element returns from parse_announce_data() — failing after 8-tuple extension with nomadnet_dest field. Being fixed incrementally with sed replacements.
- Upgrade screen WHAT'S NEW changelog empty for pipx installs FIXED in v0.13.15: new Strategy 2 downloads the target version's wheel from PyPI (~200KB), extracts its _changelog.py via zipfile, and parses entries. Target version's bundled changelog always covers itself and prior versions.
- UpgradeScreen changelog fully fixed in v0.13.15: Strategy 2 (_fetch_from_pypi_wheel) downloads target version's wheel from PyPI and extracts _changelog.py — works for all install methods including pipx. Strategy 1 (bundled) still fires first for cases where installed version already has the entries.
- _resolve_nomadnet_destination() adds hub capability heuristic in v0.13.15: if device has 'pages' capability, use its own destination_hash optimistically — page browser handles connection errors. Hub capability ('hub') alone is NOT used; only 'pages' capability triggers this fallback.
- v0.13.16 fix: caps_str was generated before NomadNet identity probe, so dynamically added 'pages' capability was not included in the announce wire format. Fixed by moving caps_str join after the NomadNet block.
- Hub pages capability fix shipped in v0.13.16: daemon now probes NomadNet identity files directly (including /app/.nomadnetwork/storage/identity) regardless of page_server.enabled, adds 'pages' capability and nomadnet_dest to announce even in multi-process hub mode.
- Pages tab in MeshDeviceDetailScreen was missing for hub v0.13.15 because page_server.is_started gate prevented NomadNet identity probe. Fixed in v0.13.16 which probes NomadNet identity files directly regardless of page_server.enabled.
- Micron renderer in page_browser produces anemic output: headings are just bold with no color, links have no color differentiation, no visual hierarchy. Improvement in progress — adding colored heading styles (#5af0ce h1, #3fbfa0 h2, #6a9a8e h3).
- v0.13.2 released: fix VIA column in exploration/dashboard now shows 'Interface Name → next_hop_hash' (e.g., 'Styrene Community Hub → a3f6f4c8') to disambiguate which transport node relayed an announce when multiple hubs are connected.
- Dashboard VIA column showed local interface name only (e.g., 'Styrene Community Hub') for all devices — fixed in v0.13.2: discovered_via now includes next-hop transport hash suffix (e.g., 'Styrene Community Hub → a3f6f4c8'), disambiguating which transport node relayed announces when multiple peers are enabled.
- Dashboard mesh devices grouped under a single interface name — partially improved in v0.13.2 by adding next-hop hash suffix to VIA label; most announces legitimately arrive through Styrene Community Hub (best connectivity), but the suffix now shows which specific transport node relayed each.
- Settings screen stacked on repeated backtick press — fixed in v0.13.4: action_push_screen_settings now checks isinstance(self.screen, SettingsScreen) and returns early if already on top.
- Chat send failure while node is actively announcing: LXMF delivery failures can occur even when destination is visible in mesh because path establishment and announce propagation are decoupled — path may not be established despite announce arriving. No UI explanation of this to the user.
- send_message returns None causing retry failures when the peer's identity is not in RNS cache or NodeStore — _resolve_identity() exhausts all 4 strategies. This is the primary cause of both initial send failures and retry failures when destination is unreachable.
- ChatWidget Phase 3 retry design: R binding calls action_retry_message → auto-selects last failed bubble if none selected → _retry_single(message_id). ctrl+r retries all failed with per-message groups. _retry_after_refresh handles message_id=0 optimistic bubbles by refreshing from DB first. message_id=0 optimistic bubble issue now addressed.
- Chat failure/retry system partially fixed: R now auto-selects last failed bubble without requiring Tab+navigation; optimistic bubbles (id=0) trigger _retry_after_refresh() which refreshes from DB first. User still needs to know R key exists — no inline UI affordance yet. Footer shows 'R Retry' only when ChatWidget (not Input) has focus.
- Failed messages now show red ✗ + '↻ press R to retry' hint on the line below. R key auto-selects last failed message if none is selected. Still no clickable button — keyboard-only affordance.
- _update_optimistic_status for attachment-only messages (empty content) was broken — content matching skipped empty string, so it matched wrong or no bubble. Fixed: empty content matches most recent pending outgoing bubble instead of content-matching.
- action_retry_all_failed was silently cancelling all-but-last retry workers because all used group='chat-retry'. Fixed: each retry worker now uses group='chat-retry-{message_id}'.
- After send failure, optimistic bubble (message_id=0) was never replaced with real DB record because _refresh_messages() was not called on the failure path. Fixed: both TimeoutError and generic Exception paths now call _refresh_messages() after 0.3s delay.
- send_message returns None when RNS.Identity.recall() evicts the identity cache for a recently-announcing peer — identity was stored during announce processing but cache flushed before send. Root cause of 'Server c0cd' chat send failures despite active announces.
- _resolve_identity Strategy 5 (path request + 10s blocking poll) blocks the IPC handler thread. Since async IPC handlers call synchronous send_message() directly (not in an executor), this risks blocking the asyncio event loop if ever called from coroutine context without thread isolation.
- UpgradeScreen changelog implemented in progress: WHAT'S NEW panel added to UpgradeScreen showing changes between current and latest. fetch_changelog() in tui/services/changelog.py uses local git log → GitHub API → PyPI version list fallback chain. GitHub compare API fails for private repo without auth; git log strategy added to resolve.
- fetch_changelog() GitHub compare API strategy fails for private repos without auth token — git log strategy (now Strategy 1) resolves this for local git checkouts.

- align-vertical:bottom removed from #chat-message-container CSS. Property was confirmed to do nothing for overflow scroll containers in Textual. Scroll gravity achieved only via scroll_end() calls.
- _update_optimistic_status was fixed to match outgoing bubbles by content (raw_content comparison) rather than just reversed position, preventing wrong-bubble updates on concurrent sends.
- _stage_clipboard_attachment dead guard (unreachable 'if attachment is not None') removed; also fixed indentation regression from the removal.
- Chat input bar separator border changed from 'heavy' to 'wide' in imperial_crt.tcss for broader terminal compatibility.

- _load_comms_data() refactored in v0.13.1 to use SQL aggregates (func.count, case) instead of loading all rows — no longer blocks event loop on large message history.
- _load_comms_data() SQL blocking fixed in v0.13.1: replaced .all() with SQL aggregate queries, no rows loaded into Python memory.
- TRAFFIC section in NodeInfoPanel IPC mode partially fixed in v0.13.1: messages_received now populated from message_count total; sent/pending remain 0 pending IPC endpoint changes for per-direction breakdown.
- _fetch_daemon_status IPC calls parallelized in v0.13.1: conversations, contacts, and auto_reply launched as asyncio.create_task concurrently, all runs use group='dashboard-status' to prevent concurrent worker races.

- RNS announce callback does not receive the receiving interface — interface info must be read post-hoc from RNS.Transport.path_table[destination_hash][5] after the callback fires, which may reflect a different announce's winner if multiple hubs compete.
- IPC handler CMD_SEND_CHAT empty-content validation fixed in v0.11.13: changed `if not req.content` to `if req.content is None` and added guard `if not req.content and not req.attachment_data_b64: return error`. Allows image-only sends.

- Ctrl+Y clipboard attach send-on-empty fixed in v0.11.13: IPC handler now accepts empty content string when attachment_data_b64 is provided. Attachment-only messages fully functional.
- IPC handler test test_handle_cmd_send_chat_requires_content renamed to test_handle_cmd_send_chat_requires_content_or_attachment with updated assertion 'content or attachment is required' to match v0.11.13 validation change.

- ChatWidget _refresh_messages() incremental re-render fully fixed in v0.12.2: only new messages appended (no full teardown+rebuild), existing bubbles preserved, date separators added between day boundaries
- ConversationScreen title bar now shows just the display name (or first 12 chars of hash), no longer shows 'CONVERSATION - fullhash'. Fixed in v0.13.0.

- Ctrl+O binding for clipboard attach is non-functional on macOS because the terminal driver intercepts Ctrl+O as DISCARD before Textual receives it (even in raw mode). Fixed in v0.11.8 by switching primary binding to ctrl+p; ctrl+o kept as hidden fallback.
- Ctrl+O binding for clipboard attach was non-functional on macOS (terminal DISCARD interception). Resolved in v0.11.8: ctrl+p is now primary visible binding, ctrl+o retained as hidden fallback.
- Textual run_worker + run_in_executor dispatches PyObjC calls to thread pool threads where macOS NSPasteboard requires the main thread — causes silent failure of clipboard reads regardless of binding used.
- Textual App.COMMAND_PALETTE_BINDING = 'ctrl+p' — using ctrl+p as a custom binding in ChatWidget conflicts with the command palette and is silently overridden. Custom bindings must avoid ctrl+p.
- PyObjC NSPasteboard.generalPasteboard() must be called from the main thread. Using asyncio run_in_executor (thread pool) causes it to silently return empty/stale data. Fixed in v0.11.10 by moving clipboard reads to synchronous calls in action handlers (which run on main thread). [2026-03-02]

## Architecture

- StyreneScreen base class (src/styrened/tui/screens/base.py, v0.16.0+): abstract _load_data(), _cleanup(), _on_error(), _loading_message() hooks. self.bridge property raises BridgeUnavailableError when None. 3-attempt retry with 0.5/1/2s exponential backoff. StyreneLoadingIndicator widget. Worker ownership: suspend/unmount cancels owned workers. All TUI screens inherit from StyreneScreen. ui_state/ package: base, nodes, sessions, daemon, config, mail, comms, page_browser, workspace modules.
- Yggdrasil + I2P integration (shipped v0.15.3–v0.15.4): DaemonAdapter ABC at services/daemon_adapter.py. YggdrasilAdapter at services/yggdrasil.py (DISABLED/ADOPT/MANAGED modes, admin socket JSON-RPC). I2PAdapter at services/i2p.py (DISABLED/ADOPT/MANAGED, HTTP proxy port 4444/4445). Config: YggdrasilConfig + I2PConfig in models/config.py. CAPABILITY_YGGDRASIL + CAPABILITY_I2P announce bits. MeshDevice.ygg_address + .b32_address fields. /meta enriched with ygg_address+ygg_port+b32_address. PageBrowserService handles NomadNet (RNS.Link), HTTPS, and .i2p (HTTP proxy) URLs. NixOS module lives in styrene-edge (not this repo).
- MeshDeviceTree (peer-browsing Tree widget) lives in ExplorationScreen (Nodes workspace) only. MY MESH groups trusted nodes (RBAC role ≥ PEER) by interface with role badges; OTHER shows anonymous nodes with truncated identity hash + auto-queried /meta (version/profile) via _fetch_meta() worker. /info queried on explicit 'i' keypress. Dashboard (Home workspace) has no peer tree — it uses NodeInfoPanel + ActivityFeedWidget only. See src/styrened/tui/screens/exploration.py.
- DaemonHarness (tests/harness/daemon.py): Spawns isolated styrened subprocesses for integration testing. Key isolation mechanisms: STYRENE_CONFIG_DIR/DATA_DIR/STATE_DIR env vars → temp dir, .paths-migrated marker prevents legacy data copy, config_path_override forces generated RNS config (prevents using user's ~/.config/reticulum), STYRENED_SOCKET for IPC. Factory: DaemonHarness.from_fixture("alpha"|"bravo"|"host"). RNS announce discovery takes ~15s on localhost TCP.
- YggdrasilAdapter wired into daemon (commit ac2bfa6): _start_yggdrasil_adapter()/_stop_yggdrasil_adapter() mirror I2P pattern. Duplicate YggdrasilConfig in services/yggdrasil.py removed — uses canonical models.config.YggdrasilConfig. Both adapters now have _find_binary() with search order: explicit config path → ~/.styrene/bin/ → system PATH → Nix store. Default mode for both is DISABLED.
- CopActivitySummary widget (src/styrened/tui/widgets/cop_activity_summary.py) replaces ActivityFeedWidget on Home COP screen. Coalesces raw IPC events into prioritized situation lines (max 6): NODE_ANOMALY → UNREAD → FILE → SECURITY → HUB → NODE_DISCOVERY. Transport-tagged via discovered_via prefix mapping (TCP, Auto, RNode, I2P, Ygg, Mesh, etc). Resolved situations dim then age out after 30m TTL. ActivityFeedWidget remains for Diagnostics tab raw firehose. 42 tests in tests/tui/widgets/test_cop_activity_summary.py.
- EventBus at src/styrened/services/event_bus.py — styrened's central nervous system. Simple async observer: subscribe(type, callback)/emit(type, action, **data). Five coarse types: node_changed, message_changed, hub_changed, link_changed, config_changed. emit() fans out via asyncio.create_task (never blocks). Auto-removes subscribers after 5 consecutive failures. Logging escalation: TRACE(5)=payload+timing, DEBUG=emit/sub, INFO=lifecycle+recovery, WARNING=slow(>500ms)+auto-remove, ERROR=exception+traceback. Daemon instance at StyreneDaemon.event_bus. TUI side: DaemonEvent Textual message (src/styrened/tui/models/events.py) posted from dashboard activity subscription, handled by screens for instant refresh. 60s reconciliation timer as fallback. Design doc: docs/internal-event-system.md
- Direct daemon launches read paths.config_file() -> ~/.config/styrene/config.yaml (see src/styrened/paths.py); editing ~/.config/styrene/core-config.yaml alone does not affect a running styrened daemon.
- HomeNodeSummaryTable now caches mesh nodes/unread state, rebuilds on resize, and shows an inline overflow row ('showing X of Y • press N for full list') when the Home COP viewport cannot display the full non-LOST set. See src/styrened/tui/widgets/home_node_summary.py.
- PageResponse.content_type field (str | None) added to page_browser.py. fetch_url() captures Content-Type from HTTP headers; fetch_page() (NomadNet) hardcodes "text/x-micron". IPC handler handle_query_page passes content_type through. _validate_meta_response in direct_link.py now allows ygg_address, ygg_port, b32_address, web_url fields through (was previously stripping overlay addresses). IdentityConfig.web_url added to config model + parser + serializer. MeshDevice.web_url field + NodeStore ALTER TABLE migration. _gather_meta includes web_url when configured. 21 tests in tests/unit/test_page_content_type.py.

## Architecture
_System structure, component relationships, key abstractions_

- Envoy Gateway HTTPRoute path allowlisting: explicitly enumerate only required paths (e.g. /realms, /resources, /js for an IdP) as route rules. Paths with no matching rule return 404 from the gateway — no backend reference means implicit drop. Admin paths are excluded by omission rather than explicit deny. [2026-03-05]
- In agentic CLI frameworks, sendMessage()-style APIs often split delivery modes (e.g. 'steer' vs 'followUp' vs 'nextTurn') with different subsets available per call site. Document which modes are available on each variant — missing options cause silent behavioral differences at runtime. [2026-03-04]

## Known Issues
_Bugs, flaky tests, workarounds_

- OAuth/OIDC implicit grant type and ROPC (password) flows enabled in an IdP (e.g. Keycloak) expose credential stuffing and token leakage attack surfaces. Both legacy grant types should be explicitly disabled in realm/tenant settings when not required; only authorization_code + PKCE should remain active. [2026-03-05]
